_meta.name: CLM r4 NVL relaunch (2nd) — slot reoccupied after 20260416 pod death

pod.name: anima-clm1b-r4-nvl-rl3
pod.note_ephemeral_risk: volumeInGb=0 — durability via R2 periodic sync (r2_sync_watcher.sh polling 60s)

prior_pod_failures_this_session.reason: SSH port 22 never opened — runpodctl create pods does NOT pass PUBLIC_KEY env, so sshd never auto-started. Pod paid idle for ~17min before terminate.
Same issue — retried with runpodctl, same missing PUBLIC_KEY env.

fix_applied: Switched pod creation from runpodctl CLI to GraphQL podFindAndDeployOnDemand with env=[{key: PUBLIC_KEY, value: <pubkey>}]. SSH auto-up on first try (under 1 min after runtime ready).

binary_rebuild.build_fix: FIX-1: tensor_kernels.c must be placed at /workspace/native/ (runtime.c line 3890 has #include "native/tensor_kernels.c"). Initial scp to /workspace/tensor_kernels.c failed; resolved by mkdir native && mv.
build_warnings: runtime.c weak_import attribute warnings (non-fatal, platform-specific ignore)

rationale_pillars: train_clm_composed.c main() does NOT expose a --resume or load-ckpt CLI arg path; binary always cold-starts from random init (seed=54321) per the [gpu_train] init_gpu_weights log line.
R2 had r4/step_5000.hexackpt (3.54 GB) from prior run but reusing it would require a resume code path that doesn't exist in the current binary.
training/CLAUDE.md rule: ckpt dir always fresh (mandatory per runpod_mfs_quota lesson).
feedback_no_resume_data_change gate: corpus + params identical, but binary can't resume, so moot.
Prior run only reached step ~2800 (6min) before dying — tiny loss compared to the 750k total; rebuilding from step 0 is cheap.
ckpt_strategy.r2_old_ckpt_status: KEPT at r2:anima-models/clm1b/r4/step_5000.hexackpt for reference (not used). If future binary adds resume support it can reuse.

cuda_linkage.nvrtc_dlopen: OK (first compile: 61309 bytes PTX of clm_kernels.cu, cached to .hexa-cache/ptx_875149335.ptx)

r2_sync.sync_method: rclone copy per-file; verify by size match; delete local on success. Skips files touched within last 10s (avoid partial write uploads).
r2_sync.last_upload_status: no ckpts yet (first save at step 5000 ~08:64Z)
r2_sync.compliance_note: Watcher runs as a separate process reading from /workspace — NOT in training process. Complies with training/CLAUDE.md runpod_mfs_quota rule.

dependencies_satisfied.hf_token_needed: N (CLM is from-scratch byte-level, no HF base model)

alm.status: RUNNING (A2-Tool retrain, not re-verified this session per task instructions)

dual_track_status.cap_status: AT_MAX (feedback_h100_authorized — do not exceed without explicit user consent)

d8_gate_impact_on_alm_70b.new_status: RE_OCCUPIED (CLM r4 slot is filled again as of 2026-04-17T08:52Z on pod abx2uqoa7qbbh8)
d8_gate_impact_on_alm_70b.rationale: CLM is a PARALLEL track per feedback_dual_track + feedback_goal_clm_alm_agi. CLM occupying 1 H100 NVL does not physically preclude 70B's 4xH100 SXM allocation (different GPU types, different slots). The D8 blocker was specifically about 'simultaneous 5-pod total exceeding caps', which is still resolved by user's 2-pod H100 auth being sufficient as long as 70B swap timing is orchestrated.
d8_gate_impact_on_alm_70b.revised_d8_wording_suggestion: CLM r4 relaunched 2026-04-17 — runs independently at 1xH100 NVL ($3.07/hr). Does NOT block 70B 4xSXM launch since they are different GPU fleets. 70B D10 allocation is the real gating step.

monitoring_cmds: ssh -i /Users/ghost/.runpod/ssh/RunPod-Key-Go -p 14655 root@216.81.245.23 'tmux attach -t clm_r4_nvl'  # live view (Ctrl-B d to detach)
known_caveats: stdout buffer flush is 4KB block-buffered — early log visibility has a ~6-7 min lag. First 410s produced 4248 bytes of log at step 2700.
phi_proxy reports -nan at step 2500 — known non-blocking issue, training proceeds.
kr_acc reports '-1/16' — placeholder, not a real metric yet.

Ephemeral workspace (volumeInGb=0) — catastrophic data loss if container restarts BEFORE first R2 sync at step ~5000 (~6min from now). This risk was CATASTROPHIC in original 20260416 pod death. Mitigated by r2_sync_watcher's 60s poll.

H100 auth cap of 2 is now AT_MAX. 70B launch (4xH100 SXM) will require either (a) CLM r4 to finish first (~32h), (b) explicit user OK to exceed cap, or (c) different GPU fleet which 70B already plans (SXM not NVL).

growth_loop_status.note: Per feedback_report_includes_loop — growth_loop not directly checked in this relaunch session (focused on dual track CLM revival). Launched CLM r4 restores the paired CLM+ALM parallel mandate.
